step_by_step_reasoning: We identify two distinct user interactions: the initial ad auction (WWW) and the subsequent conversion event. The architecture divides these into a high-performance bidding layer and a tracking layer running on EC2 Spot. High-speed lookup is achieved using Redis (ElastiCache) for data younger than 1 hour. For older events, data is archived to S3 (with a Lambda transforming JSON to Parquet) and queried via Athena triggered by SQS and a helper Lambda.

graph.name: Smadex Programmatic Advertising Architecture
graph.notes: Hybrid hot/cold data storage architecture designed for low latency and cost optimization, processing 1 million requests per second.

nodes.name: Web/App Users
nodes.notes: Initiates ad requests and interacts with ads.

nodes.name: Bidding Servers (Spot)
nodes.notes: Handles 1M/sec bid requests within 100ms latency budget.

nodes.name: Tracking Servers (Spot)
nodes.notes: Tracks post-bid user interactions using unique IDs.

nodes.name: ElastiCache Redis
nodes.notes: In-memory cache holding hot bid events (1-hour TTL).

nodes.name: Amazon S3
nodes.notes: Raw and optimized historical bid data storage.

nodes.name: Lambda Parquet Converter
nodes.notes: Transforms JSON data to Parquet format to optimize Athena query costs.

nodes.name: Amazon SQS
nodes.notes: Queues tracking events older than 1 hour for cold-path resolution.

nodes.name: Lambda Query Executor
nodes.notes: Triggered by SQS to invoke Athena queries.

nodes.name: Amazon Athena
nodes.notes: Queries historical cold data in S3 using partitioned Parquet files.

edges.notes: Dispatches bid requests during web load.
Writes bid ID to Redis with 1h TTL.
Flushes bid data every minute as JSON.
Triggers converter on new file arrival.
Sends user interaction events with ID.
Looks up IDs for events < 1 hour old.
Scans S3 bucket to match conversion IDs.
